evals.prompt: I need to train a binary text classifier on data.csv. The text is in the 'review' column and the label (0 or 1) is in the 'sentiment' column.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, reads the CSV, performs Stage 1 data analysis, reports class distribution and text statistics, then proceeds to Stage 2 model scheme.
expectations: The skill correctly identifies text_col='review' and label_col='sentiment'
Stage 1 data analysis is executed and stats are reported to the user
Stage 2 model scheme is generated and presented with priorities

evals.prompt: Help me train a spam classifier. Text is in the message column, label is in is_spam column (0=not spam, 1=spam). Data is at spam_data.csv. I want to try both traditional ML and deep learning.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, confirms column names, proceeds through all 4 stages. In Stage 2, includes both traditional ML and deep learning models. Stage 3 trains models from both categories.
expectations: Skill correctly identifies this as a binary text classification task
Stage 2 scheme includes both traditional ML and deep learning models
Stage 3 trains or attempts training of both model categories
GPU is auto-detected and used if available

evals.prompt: Build a sentiment classifier that determines if product reviews are positive or negative. Use deep learning with GloVe embeddings and also try BERT. I have product_reviews.csv with text in 'review_text' and label in 'rating' (0=negative, 1=positive).
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, guides user through the pipeline. Stage 2 includes deep learning (CNN/LSTM/GRU) and BERT variants. Training results include both model categories. Final output has deployment artifacts.
expectations: Stage 2 scheme includes deep learning models with GloVe embeddings
Stage 2 scheme includes transformer models (BERT variants)
